title: How Content Cucumber and Portal IQ Collaborate on HubSpot Audits
excerpt: Content Cucumber and Portal IQ run two complementary layers of the same HubSpot engagement. Portal IQ delivers an automated, HubSpot App Marketplace certified portal audit. Content Cucumber adds the content, schema, and AEO layer around it. Together the two teams cover what a HubSpot operator actually needs in one offering.
content:
A HubSpot operator has two questions to answer. Is the HubSpot portal itself set up to perform, and is the content around it discoverable and citable. Neither question is fully answered by one team alone. Portal IQ and Content Cucumber collaborate so a HubSpot client can have both layers covered without juggling two procurement conversations.

This is the working model.
challenge: HubSpot is a platform that rewards two very different kinds of work. The first is portal configuration, the data hygiene, automation, and app integration that makes Marketing, Sales, and Service Hubs perform. The second is the content and discoverability layer, the schema, the AEO writing, the citability that gets a HubSpot brand named by AI engines and found in search. Most agencies do one well. A client who needs both has to assemble it from separate vendors and connect the pieces themselves.
approach: Content Cucumber and Portal IQ split the work along the natural seam between the two layers and meet a HubSpot client with one combined offering. Portal IQ runs its automated HubSpot audit, a HubSpot App Marketplace certified product that scans Marketing, Sales, Service, and the CRM and returns a prioritized set of recommendations. Content Cucumber, as a HubSpot Solutions Partner, runs the content and discoverability layer around it: schema, llms.txt, citable content, AEO. The two teams keep their own brands, their own contracts, and their own work surfaces. Content Cucumber already resells Portal IQ partner-branded audits as part of its HubSpot Solutions Partner offering, so the collaboration is live and operational, not a pitch deck.
results: A HubSpot client engaging the combined offering gets one engagement and two layers. Portal IQ's audit covers portal health, configuration, and app use. Content Cucumber's layer covers the content, schema, and AEO work that turns a healthy portal into a brand AI engines name. The two layers do not overlap and are not bundled by reselling one as the other. Each side carries its own deliverable under its own brand, with reciprocal referral running in both directions. Co-sell is the lead shape; white-label of Portal IQ partner-branded audits through Content Cucumber is the second working shape and is already live.
